text.title: Accessibility-First Design: Figma Audit Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic Figma-API audit workflow for design-phase accessibility: subagent roles, JSON output schema for frame auditing, and LLM gotchas when generating or reviewing accessible designs.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Run a Figma-plugin-driven audit before handoff: subagent A pulls frames via Figma REST API, subagent B runs contrast/target/focus checks, subagent C generates a remediation list with specific frame IDs and proposed fixes. Loop with the designer; do not merge fixes blindly. Engineering handoff includes the audit report so developers know what has and has not been verified.

rule.statement: Never apply automated contrast or target-size fixes without designer review; fixes often break visual hierarchy (e.g., darkening a brand color to pass AA).
rule.rationale: Automated fixes optimize for the accessibility metric, not for design intent. A darkened brand primary may pass contrast but invalidate the brand. Fixes must be flagged for designer decision, not silently applied.

example.note: Figma frame audit: "For each Figma frame, output JSON: { frame_id, issues: [{ rule, severity, element_id, current, required, fix_hint }] }. Rules: contrast (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large), target_size (>=44x44), focus_visible, color_only, motion_pause, time_limit, semantic_handoff_present. Severity: blocker (WCAG 2.2 AA fail) | warn | info."

antipattern.description: LLMs hallucinating WCAG criterion numbers (e.g., citing "WCAG 1.4.13" for an issue that is actually 1.4.11).
antipattern.reason: Agents are prone to criterion number errors. Constrain to a pre-validated rule list and require the agent to cite the exact SC number and name. Reviewers will catch and reject incorrect citations.

antipattern.description: Estimating contrast ratios from screenshots rather than extracting hex values from tokens or the DOM.
antipattern.reason: Screenshot-based contrast estimation is unreliable due to JPEG compression, rendering engine differences, and anti-aliasing. Always extract actual color values programmatically.

antipattern.description: Generating false positives for "color not the only indicator" on icons that already have adjacent labels.
antipattern.reason: Agents apply this rule mechanically without understanding that a color-coded icon with a text label already satisfies SC 1.4.1. Provide context including adjacent element content.

rule.statement: Track accessibility issues found at design stage versus handoff stage versus production; the ratio of design-stage finds to later-stage finds is the shift-left KPI.
rule.rationale: If most finds are in production rather than design, the design-phase process is not working. The KPI makes the shift-left investment visible and improvable.
